description: Curated space and astronomy facts for debate fact-checking. Includes verified facts and common misconceptions.

facts.claim: The Sun is a medium-sized yellow dwarf star classified as a G-type main-sequence star
The Sun is the largest star in the Milky Way galaxy
facts.correction: The Sun is a relatively small star. Stars like UY Scuti and Betelgeuse are far larger.

facts.claim: Light from the Sun takes approximately 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth
The Sun accounts for about 99.86 percent of the total mass of the solar system
Mercury is the hottest planet in the solar system because it is closest to the Sun
facts.correction: Venus is the hottest planet due to its thick atmosphere and greenhouse effect, despite being farther from the Sun than Mercury.

facts.claim: Venus rotates in the opposite direction to most other planets, meaning the Sun rises in the west on Venus
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus
Venus has a thick atmosphere composed mainly of carbon dioxide with clouds of sulfuric acid
Mars is known as the Red Planet because of iron oxide or rust on its surface
Mars has two small moons named Phobos and Deimos
Olympus Mons on Mars is the tallest known volcano and mountain in the solar system at about 21.9 kilometers high
Mars has a similar atmospheric pressure to Earth making it relatively easy to breathe there
facts.correction: Mars has less than 1% of Earth's atmospheric pressure, and its atmosphere is 95% carbon dioxide. Humans cannot breathe there without life support.

facts.claim: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system with a mass more than twice that of all other planets combined
Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a massive storm that has been raging for at least 350 years
Jupiter has at least 95 known moons
Europa, one of Jupiter's moons, likely has a subsurface ocean of liquid water beneath its icy crust
Saturn is the only planet in the solar system with rings
facts.correction: Jupiter, Uranus, and Neptune also have ring systems, though Saturn's are the most prominent and visible.

facts.claim: Saturn is less dense than water and could theoretically float if placed in a large enough body of water
Saturn's moon Titan has a thick atmosphere and liquid methane lakes on its surface
Titan is the only moon in the solar system known to have a dense atmosphere
Uranus rotates on its side with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees
Neptune has the strongest sustained winds of any planet in the solar system reaching up to 2100 kilometers per hour
Pluto is classified as a dwarf planet since its reclassification by the IAU in 2006
Pluto is smaller than Earth's Moon
The Moon is slowly moving away from Earth at a rate of about 3.8 centimeters per year
The dark side of the Moon is always dark and never receives sunlight
facts.correction: The far side of the Moon receives just as much sunlight as the near side. It is called the far side because it always faces away from Earth, not because it is dark.

facts.claim: The Moon has no atmosphere at all
facts.correction: The Moon has an extremely thin atmosphere called an exosphere, composed of helium, neon, and argon, but it is so thin it is nearly a vacuum.

facts.claim: The Asteroid Belt between Mars and Jupiter contains millions of asteroids
The Asteroid Belt is so densely packed that spacecraft have to carefully navigate through it
facts.correction: The Asteroid Belt is mostly empty space. The average distance between asteroids is about 1 million kilometers. Spacecraft pass through it without any special navigation.

facts.claim: The Kuiper Belt is a region of icy bodies beyond Neptune's orbit that includes Pluto
The Oort Cloud is a hypothesized spherical cloud of icy objects surrounding the solar system at a distance of up to 100,000 AU
Comets are composed of ice, dust, and rocky material, often described as dirty snowballs
Halley's Comet is visible from Earth approximately every 75 to 79 years
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second
Nothing can travel faster than the speed of light according to Einstein's theory of special relativity
A light-year is a unit of time equal to one year at the speed of light
facts.correction: A light-year is a unit of distance, not time. It is the distance that light travels in one year, approximately 9.46 trillion kilometers.

facts.claim: The Milky Way galaxy is a barred spiral galaxy approximately 100,000 light-years in diameter
The Milky Way contains an estimated 100 to 400 billion stars
The supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way is called Sagittarius A-star
The Andromeda Galaxy is the nearest major galaxy to the Milky Way at about 2.5 million light-years away
The Milky Way and Andromeda Galaxy are expected to collide in about 4.5 billion years
There are approximately 2 trillion galaxies in the observable universe
The observable universe is approximately 93 billion light-years in diameter
The universe is approximately 13.8 billion years old based on observations of the cosmic microwave background
The Big Bang theory states that the universe began as an explosion in empty space
facts.correction: The Big Bang was not an explosion in space. It was the rapid expansion of space itself from an extremely hot and dense state. There was no pre-existing empty space for it to explode into.

facts.claim: Dark matter makes up about 27 percent of the universe while dark energy makes up about 68 percent
Ordinary visible matter makes up only about 5 percent of the total content of the universe
The cosmic microwave background radiation is the afterglow of the Big Bang detectable in every direction in space
The universe is expanding and the rate of expansion is accelerating
facts.source: Nobel Prize in Physics 2011, Perlmutter, Schmidt, Riess

facts.claim: Black holes are objects so dense that nothing, not even light, can escape their gravitational pull once past the event horizon
Black holes suck in everything around them like cosmic vacuum cleaners
facts.correction: Black holes do not suck things in. They have the same gravitational pull as any object of the same mass. Objects must cross the event horizon to be trapped. The Sun replaced by a same-mass black hole would not pull the planets in.

facts.claim: Stellar black holes form when massive stars collapse at the end of their life cycle
The first image of a black hole shadow was captured in 2019 showing the supermassive black hole in galaxy M87
Neutron stars are the densest known objects in the universe, with a teaspoon of neutron star material weighing about 6 billion tons
A supernova is the explosive death of a massive star and can briefly outshine an entire galaxy
All stars eventually become black holes when they die
facts.correction: Only the most massive stars become black holes. Medium-mass stars become white dwarfs, and somewhat larger stars become neutron stars. Our Sun will become a white dwarf.

facts.claim: Red dwarf stars are the most common type of star in the Milky Way
Stars twinkle because of turbulence in Earth's atmosphere, not because of any property of the stars themselves
The North Star Polaris is the brightest star in the night sky
facts.correction: Sirius is the brightest star in the night sky. Polaris is only about the 50th brightest star, but it is important for navigation because it is very close to the north celestial pole.

facts.claim: Betelgeuse is a red supergiant star in the constellation Orion that is expected to explode as a supernova within the next million years
The International Space Station orbits Earth at an altitude of approximately 400 kilometers
The ISS travels at approximately 28,000 kilometers per hour and completes one orbit of Earth roughly every 90 minutes
The Apollo 11 mission in 1969 was the first to land humans on the Moon with Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin
There have been six successful crewed Moon landings all part of the NASA Apollo program
Yuri Gagarin was the first human in space aboard Vostok 1 on April 12, 1961
Valentina Tereshkova was the first woman in space aboard Vostok 6 in 1963
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched in 1990 and orbits Earth at about 547 kilometers altitude
The James Webb Space Telescope launched in December 2021 and orbits the Sun at the L2 Lagrange point about 1.5 million kilometers from Earth
Voyager 1 is the farthest human-made object from Earth, having entered interstellar space in 2012
Voyager 1 carries a Golden Record containing sounds and images of Earth intended for any extraterrestrial life that might find it
The Space Shuttle program ran from 1981 to 2011 with 135 missions
SpaceX's Falcon 9 was the first orbital-class rocket to successfully land and be reused
The Mars Perseverance rover landed on Mars in February 2021 and carried the Ingenuity helicopter
Ingenuity was the first powered controlled flight on another planet
There is no gravity in space or on the International Space Station
facts.correction: Gravity exists everywhere in space. Astronauts on the ISS experience microgravity because they are in continuous freefall around Earth, not because there is no gravity. Gravity at ISS altitude is about 90% of surface gravity.

facts.claim: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye
facts.correction: The Great Wall of China is not visible from low Earth orbit with the naked eye. It is too narrow. This is a common myth that has been debunked by multiple astronauts.

facts.claim: In space, without a spacesuit, a human would instantly freeze
facts.correction: Space is a vacuum with very poor heat transfer. A human would not instantly freeze. The main dangers are lack of oxygen, ebullism (body fluids boiling at low pressure), and decompression. Freezing would take a long time.

facts.claim: Sound cannot travel through the vacuum of space because there are no molecules to carry sound waves
Earth's atmosphere protects us from most meteoroids which burn up as meteors before reaching the surface
The largest known structure in the universe is the Hercules-Corona Borealis Great Wall at about 10 billion light-years across
Exoplanets are planets that orbit stars outside our solar system and thousands have been confirmed
The first exoplanet orbiting a Sun-like star was 51 Pegasi b discovered in 1995
The TRAPPIST-1 system contains seven Earth-sized rocky planets, three of which are in the habitable zone
The habitable zone is the region around a star where conditions might allow liquid water to exist on a planet's surface
Proxima Centauri b is the closest known exoplanet to Earth orbiting the nearest star to the Sun at about 4.24 light-years away
The Kepler Space Telescope discovered the majority of known exoplanets before being retired in 2018
Gravitational waves were first directly detected in 2015 by the LIGO observatory confirming Einstein's prediction
Pulsars are rapidly rotating neutron stars that emit beams of electromagnetic radiation
The Chandrasekhar limit of about 1.4 solar masses is the maximum mass of a stable white dwarf star
Our solar system is located in the Orion Arm of the Milky Way galaxy about 26,000 light-years from the galactic center
Enceladus, a moon of Saturn, has geysers that shoot water ice into space from a subsurface ocean
Io, a moon of Jupiter, is the most volcanically active body in the solar system
Ganymede is the largest moon in the solar system, even larger than the planet Mercury
The Crab Nebula is the remnant of a supernova explosion observed by Chinese astronomers in 1054 AD
White dwarfs are the remnant cores of low to medium mass stars that have exhausted their nuclear fuel
The Sun will eventually become a red giant in about 5 billion years before shedding its outer layers and becoming a white dwarf
Nuclear fusion in the Sun's core converts hydrogen into helium releasing enormous amounts of energy
The solar wind is a stream of charged particles released from the Sun's corona that extends throughout the solar system
Solar flares and coronal mass ejections can disrupt satellites, communications, and power grids on Earth
The auroras or northern and southern lights are caused by charged particles from the Sun interacting with Earth's magnetic field
Earth is the only planet in the solar system known to have plate tectonics
Venus is sometimes called Earth's twin because of their similar size and mass
Mercury has virtually no atmosphere and experiences extreme temperature swings from minus 180 to 430 degrees Celsius
Mars once had liquid water flowing on its surface based on evidence from rovers and orbiters
The largest canyon in the solar system is Valles Marineris on Mars stretching over 4000 kilometers
Jupiter's moon Europa has more water than all of Earth's oceans combined
The Hubble Deep Field image revealed thousands of galaxies in a tiny patch of sky previously thought to be empty
Redshift of distant galaxies provides evidence that the universe is expanding
Dark energy is the mysterious force driving the accelerating expansion of the universe
The cosmic microwave background has a temperature of approximately 2.7 Kelvin
Quasars are the brightest objects in the universe powered by supermassive black holes actively consuming matter
The Parker Solar Probe is the fastest human-made object reaching speeds over 600,000 kilometers per hour
The Artemis program aims to return humans to the Moon and establish a sustained presence there
Seasons on Earth are caused by the planet's distance from the Sun varying throughout the year
facts.correction: Seasons are caused by Earth's axial tilt of 23.5 degrees, not by distance from the Sun. In fact, Earth is closest to the Sun during the Northern Hemisphere's winter.

facts.claim: Earth's axial tilt of approximately 23.5 degrees is responsible for the seasons
The Moon causes tides on Earth through its gravitational pull
A full Moon occurs roughly every 29.5 days which is the length of one lunar cycle
Solar eclipses happen when the Moon passes between the Sun and Earth blocking sunlight
We always see the same side of the Moon because it is tidally locked to Earth
Triton, Neptune's largest moon, orbits in the opposite direction to Neptune's rotation suggesting it was captured
The Perseid meteor shower occurs annually in August when Earth passes through debris from Comet Swift-Tuttle
Ceres is the largest object in the asteroid belt and is classified as a dwarf planet
The New Horizons spacecraft performed the first flyby of Pluto in July 2015
There are more stars in the observable universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches
Magnetars are neutron stars with extremely powerful magnetic fields trillions of times stronger than Earth's
The Drake Equation estimates the number of active communicative extraterrestrial civilizations in the Milky Way
Water has been found on the Moon in permanently shadowed craters near the poles
The Fermi Paradox highlights the contradiction between the high probability of extraterrestrial civilizations and the lack of evidence for them
GPS satellites must account for both special and general relativity to maintain accuracy
The event horizon of a black hole is the boundary beyond which nothing can escape
Hawking radiation is theoretical radiation predicted to be emitted by black holes causing them to slowly evaporate over time
The observable universe contains roughly 10 to the power of 80 atoms
Mercury is the smallest planet in the solar system
Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun
Humans have never landed on the Moon
facts.correction: Humans have landed on the Moon six times during the Apollo program between 1969 and 1972. Apollo 11 was the first crewed lunar landing.

facts.claim: We have never been to the Moon
facts.correction: NASA's Apollo program successfully landed 12 astronauts on the Moon across six missions from 1969 to 1972.

facts.claim: No human has ever walked on the Moon
facts.correction: Twelve astronauts walked on the Moon during the Apollo missions. Neil Armstrong was the first in July 1969.

facts.claim: Our solar system is the only solar system in the universe
facts.correction: There are billions of other star systems in our galaxy alone. Over 5,000 exoplanets have been confirmed orbiting other stars.

facts.claim: Our Sun is the only star with planets orbiting it
facts.correction: Thousands of exoplanets have been discovered orbiting other stars. Most stars likely have planetary systems.

facts.claim: Jupiter is the smallest planet in the solar system
facts.correction: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system, not the smallest. Mercury is the smallest planet.

facts.claim: Jupiter is a small planet
facts.correction: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system with a mass more than twice that of all other planets combined.

facts.claim: The Sun is the biggest star in the universe
facts.correction: The Sun is a medium-sized star. Many stars are far larger, including UY Scuti, Betelgeuse, and VY Canis Majoris.

facts.claim: Earth is the only planet with water
facts.correction: Water exists in many forms across the solar system. Europa and Enceladus have subsurface oceans, Mars has ice caps, and water vapor has been detected on multiple planets and moons.

facts.claim: The Earth is flat
facts.correction: Earth is an oblate spheroid, slightly flattened at the poles and bulging at the equator. This has been confirmed by countless observations, satellite imagery, and space missions.

facts.claim: Earth is the center of the universe
facts.correction: Earth orbits the Sun, which is one of hundreds of billions of stars in the Milky Way. The universe has no known center.

facts.claim: The Moon landing was faked
facts.correction: All six Apollo Moon landings were real. Evidence includes hundreds of kilograms of lunar samples, retroreflectors left on the surface, and independent verification by multiple countries.

facts.claim: Mars is too far away to ever send humans there
facts.correction: Multiple space agencies and companies are actively developing crewed Mars missions. The Artemis program and SpaceX Starship are working toward this goal.

facts.claim: There are eight planets in our solar system
The planets in order from the Sun are Mercury Venus Earth Mars Jupiter Saturn Uranus and Neptune
Earth is the third planet from the Sun
The Sun is a star not a planet
The Moon orbits Earth roughly once every 27.3 days
Mars has the largest volcano in the solar system called Olympus Mons
Saturn has the most visible and prominent ring system of any planet
The nearest star to Earth besides the Sun is Proxima Centauri at about 4.24 light-years away
Astronauts on the ISS experience weightlessness because they are in freefall around Earth not because there is no gravity
Space is completely empty with nothing in it
facts.correction: Space is not empty. It contains gas, dust, radiation, dark matter, and dark energy. Even the voids between galaxies contain a few atoms per cubic meter.

facts.claim: The universe will last forever and never end
facts.correction: Current theories suggest the universe may end through heat death, the Big Rip, or the Big Crunch, though this would be trillions of years in the future.

facts.claim: We can see all of the universe from Earth
facts.correction: We can only observe the observable universe, about 93 billion light-years in diameter. The full universe may be much larger or even infinite.

facts.claim: Mercury is the first planet from the Sun
Mercury has a diameter of approximately 4879 kilometers
Mercury is classified as a terrestrial planet
Mercury has no natural moons
A year on Mercury lasts approximately 88 Earth days
Mercury has a mean surface temperature of about 167 degrees Celsius
Mercury is about 0.39 astronomical units from the Sun
Mercury does not have rings
Mercury has the most eccentric orbit of any planet
Mercury has 28 known moons
facts.correction: Mercury has 0 known moons, not 28.

facts.claim: Mercury is the second planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Mercury is the first planet from the Sun, not the second.

facts.claim: Mercury is a gas giant planet
facts.correction: Mercury is classified as a terrestrial planet.

facts.claim: Venus is the second planet from the Sun
Venus has a diameter of approximately 12104 kilometers
Venus is classified as a terrestrial planet
Venus has no natural moons
A year on Venus lasts approximately 225 Earth days
Venus has a mean surface temperature of about 464 degrees Celsius
Venus is about 0.72 astronomical units from the Sun
Venus does not have rings
Venus rotates backwards compared to most planets
Venus has 28 known moons
facts.correction: Venus has 0 known moons, not 28.

facts.claim: Venus is the fourth planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Venus is the second planet from the Sun, not the fourth.

facts.claim: Venus is a gas giant planet
facts.correction: Venus is classified as a terrestrial planet.

facts.claim: Earth has a diameter of approximately 12756 kilometers
Earth is classified as a terrestrial planet
Earth has 1 known moon
A year on Earth lasts approximately 365.25 Earth days
Earth has a mean surface temperature of about 15 degrees Celsius
Earth is about 1.0 astronomical units from the Sun
Earth does not have rings
Earth is the densest planet in the solar system
Earth has 0 known moons
facts.correction: Earth has 1 known moon, not 0.

facts.claim: Earth is the second planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Earth is the third planet from the Sun, not the second.

facts.claim: Earth is a gas giant planet
facts.correction: Earth is classified as a terrestrial planet.

facts.claim: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun
Mars has a diameter of approximately 6792 kilometers
Mars is classified as a terrestrial planet
Mars has 2 known moons
A year on Mars lasts approximately 687 Earth days
Mars has a mean surface temperature of about -65 degrees Celsius
Mars is about 1.52 astronomical units from the Sun
Mars does not have rings
Mars has the tallest volcano in the solar system called Olympus Mons
Mars has 28 known moons
facts.correction: Mars has 2 known moons, not 28.

facts.claim: Mars is the seventh planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun, not the seventh.

facts.claim: Mars is a gas giant planet
facts.correction: Mars is classified as a terrestrial planet.

facts.claim: Jupiter is the fifth planet from the Sun
Jupiter has a diameter of approximately 142984 kilometers
Jupiter is classified as a gas giant planet
Jupiter has 95 known moons
A year on Jupiter lasts approximately 4333 Earth days
Jupiter has a mean surface temperature of about -110 degrees Celsius
Jupiter is about 5.2 astronomical units from the Sun
Jupiter has a ring system
Jupiter has the shortest day of any planet at under 10 hours
Jupiter has 146 known moons
facts.correction: Jupiter has 95 known moons, not 146.

facts.claim: Jupiter is the fourth planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Jupiter is the fifth planet from the Sun, not the fourth.

facts.claim: Jupiter is a terrestrial planet
facts.correction: Jupiter is classified as a gas giant planet.

facts.claim: Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun
Saturn has a diameter of approximately 120536 kilometers
Saturn is classified as a gas giant planet
Saturn has 146 known moons
A year on Saturn lasts approximately 10759 Earth days
Saturn has a mean surface temperature of about -140 degrees Celsius
Saturn is about 9.54 astronomical units from the Sun
Saturn has a ring system
Saturn is less dense than water
Saturn has 0 known moons
facts.correction: Saturn has 146 known moons, not 0.

facts.claim: Saturn is the first planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun, not the first.

facts.claim: Saturn is a terrestrial planet
facts.correction: Saturn is classified as a gas giant planet.

facts.claim: Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun
Uranus has a diameter of approximately 51118 kilometers
Uranus is classified as a ice giant planet
Uranus has 28 known moons
A year on Uranus lasts approximately 30687 Earth days
Uranus has a mean surface temperature of about -195 degrees Celsius
Uranus is about 19.2 astronomical units from the Sun
Uranus has a ring system
Uranus has 0 known moons
facts.correction: Uranus has 28 known moons, not 0.

facts.claim: Uranus is the fifth planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun, not the fifth.

facts.claim: Uranus is a terrestrial planet
facts.correction: Uranus is classified as a ice giant planet.

facts.claim: Neptune is the eighth planet from the Sun
Neptune has a diameter of approximately 49528 kilometers
Neptune is classified as a ice giant planet
Neptune has 16 known moons
A year on Neptune lasts approximately 60190 Earth days
Neptune has a mean surface temperature of about -200 degrees Celsius
Neptune is about 30.06 astronomical units from the Sun
Neptune has a ring system
Neptune has the strongest winds in the solar system
Neptune has 95 known moons
facts.correction: Neptune has 16 known moons, not 95.

facts.claim: Neptune is the second planet from the Sun
facts.correction: Neptune is the eighth planet from the Sun, not the second.

facts.claim: Neptune is a gas giant planet
facts.correction: Neptune is classified as a ice giant planet.

facts.claim: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system
Venus is the hottest planet in the solar system with a surface temperature of about 464 degrees Celsius
Neptune is the coldest planet in the solar system
Saturn has the most known moons of any planet with 146
Mars is larger than Neptune
facts.correction: Neptune is larger than Mars. Neptune has a diameter of 49528 km vs Mars's 6792 km.

facts.claim: Mercury is larger than Uranus
facts.correction: Uranus is larger than Mercury. Uranus has a diameter of 51118 km vs Mercury's 4879 km.

facts.claim: Earth is larger than Saturn
facts.correction: Saturn is larger than Earth. Saturn has a diameter of 120536 km vs Earth's 12756 km.

facts.claim: Moon is a moon of Earth
Moon has a diameter of approximately 3475 kilometers
Moon is notable because it is the only natural satellite of Earth
Moon is a moon of Mars
facts.correction: Moon is a moon of Earth, not Mars.

facts.claim: Phobos is a moon of Mars
Phobos has a diameter of approximately 22.4 kilometers
Phobos is notable because it is the larger of Mars's two moons
Phobos is a moon of Jupiter
facts.correction: Phobos is a moon of Mars, not Jupiter.

facts.claim: Deimos is a moon of Mars
Deimos has a diameter of approximately 12.4 kilometers
Deimos is notable because it is the smaller of Mars's two moons
Deimos is a moon of Jupiter
facts.correction: Deimos is a moon of Mars, not Jupiter.

facts.claim: Io is a moon of Jupiter
Io has a diameter of approximately 3643 kilometers
Io is notable because it is the most volcanically active body in the solar system
Io is a moon of Neptune
facts.correction: Io is a moon of Jupiter, not Neptune.

facts.claim: Europa is a moon of Jupiter
Europa has a diameter of approximately 3122 kilometers
Europa is notable because it is the has a subsurface ocean beneath its icy crust
Europa is a moon of Saturn
facts.correction: Europa is a moon of Jupiter, not Saturn.

facts.claim: Ganymede is a moon of Jupiter
Ganymede has a diameter of approximately 5268 kilometers
Ganymede is notable because it is the largest moon in the solar system
Ganymede is a moon of Saturn
facts.correction: Ganymede is a moon of Jupiter, not Saturn.

facts.claim: Callisto is a moon of Jupiter
Callisto has a diameter of approximately 4821 kilometers
Callisto is notable because it is the most heavily cratered object in the solar system
Callisto is a moon of Earth
facts.correction: Callisto is a moon of Jupiter, not Earth.

facts.claim: Titan is a moon of Saturn
Titan has a diameter of approximately 5150 kilometers
Titan is notable because it is the only moon with a dense atmosphere and liquid lakes on its surface
Titan is a moon of Jupiter
facts.correction: Titan is a moon of Saturn, not Jupiter.

facts.claim: Enceladus is a moon of Saturn
Enceladus has a diameter of approximately 504 kilometers
Enceladus is notable because it is the has geysers shooting water ice into space
Enceladus is a moon of Neptune
facts.correction: Enceladus is a moon of Saturn, not Neptune.

facts.claim: Triton is a moon of Neptune
Triton has a diameter of approximately 2707 kilometers
Triton is notable because it is the orbits Neptune in the opposite direction suggesting it was captured
Triton is a moon of Mars
facts.correction: Triton is a moon of Neptune, not Mars.

facts.claim: Charon is a moon of Pluto
Charon has a diameter of approximately 1212 kilometers
Charon is notable because it is the largest moon relative to its parent body
Charon is a moon of Earth
facts.correction: Charon is a moon of Pluto, not Earth.

facts.claim: Sun is a G-type main-sequence star
Sun is notable because it is center of our solar system
Sun is a F-type supergiant star
facts.correction: Sun is a G-type main-sequence star.

facts.claim: Proxima Centauri is a red dwarf star
Proxima Centauri is approximately 4.24 light-years from Earth
Proxima Centauri is notable because it is nearest star to the Sun
Proxima Centauri is a A-type main-sequence star
facts.correction: Proxima Centauri is a red dwarf star.

facts.claim: Sirius is a A-type main-sequence star
Sirius is approximately 8.6 light-years from Earth
Sirius is notable because it is brightest star in the night sky
Sirius is a G-type main-sequence star
facts.correction: Sirius is a A-type main-sequence star.

facts.claim: Betelgeuse is a red supergiant star
Betelgeuse is approximately 700 light-years from Earth
Betelgeuse is notable because it is one of the largest known stars expected to go supernova
Betelgeuse is a blue supergiant star
facts.correction: Betelgeuse is a red supergiant star.

facts.claim: Rigel is a blue supergiant star
Rigel is approximately 860 light-years from Earth
Rigel is notable because it is brightest star in Orion
Rigel is a red dwarf star
facts.correction: Rigel is a blue supergiant star.

facts.claim: Vega is a A-type main-sequence star
Vega is approximately 25 light-years from Earth
Vega is notable because it is was the northern pole star around 12000 BC
Vega is a G-type main-sequence star
facts.correction: Vega is a A-type main-sequence star.

facts.claim: Polaris is a F-type supergiant star
Polaris is approximately 433 light-years from Earth
Polaris is notable because it is current North Star used for navigation
Polaris is a red dwarf star
facts.correction: Polaris is a F-type supergiant star.

facts.claim: Alpha Centauri A is a G-type main-sequence star
Alpha Centauri A is approximately 4.37 light-years from Earth
Alpha Centauri A is notable because it is part of the closest star system to the Sun
Alpha Centauri A is a blue supergiant star
facts.correction: Alpha Centauri A is a G-type main-sequence star.

facts.claim: The Apollo 11 mission was launched in 1969 by NASA
Apollo 11 targeted Moon and its achievement was first crewed Moon landing
The Apollo 11 mission was launched in 1972
facts.correction: Apollo 11 was launched in 1969, not 1972.

facts.claim: The Voyager 1 mission was launched in 1977 by NASA
Voyager 1 targeted outer solar system and its achievement was farthest human-made object from Earth, entered interstellar space in 2012
The Voyager 1 mission was launched in 1987
facts.correction: Voyager 1 was launched in 1977, not 1987.

facts.claim: The Voyager 2 mission was launched in 1977 by NASA
Voyager 2 targeted outer solar system and its achievement was only spacecraft to visit all four outer planets
The Voyager 2 mission was launched in 1980
facts.correction: Voyager 2 was launched in 1977, not 1980.

facts.claim: The Hubble Space Telescope mission was launched in 1990 by NASA/ESA
Hubble Space Telescope targeted Earth orbit and its achievement was revolutionized astronomy with deep field images
The Hubble Space Telescope mission was launched in 2000
facts.correction: Hubble Space Telescope was launched in 1990, not 2000.

facts.claim: The Mars Pathfinder mission was launched in 1997 by NASA
Mars Pathfinder targeted Mars and its achievement was first successful Mars rover mission with Sojourner
The Mars Pathfinder mission was launched in 1994
facts.correction: Mars Pathfinder was launched in 1997, not 1994.

facts.claim: The Cassini-Huygens mission was launched in 1997 by NASA/ESA
Cassini-Huygens targeted Saturn and its achievement was orbited Saturn for 13 years and landed Huygens probe on Titan
The Cassini-Huygens mission was launched in 1992
facts.correction: Cassini-Huygens was launched in 1997, not 1992.

facts.claim: The Spirit and Opportunity mission was launched in 2004 by NASA
Spirit and Opportunity targeted Mars and its achievement was twin rovers that found evidence of past water on Mars
The Spirit and Opportunity mission was launched in 1999
facts.correction: Spirit and Opportunity was launched in 2004, not 1999.

facts.claim: The New Horizons mission was launched in 2006 by NASA
New Horizons targeted Pluto and its achievement was first spacecraft to fly by Pluto in 2015
The New Horizons mission was launched in 2003

facts.claim: The Curiosity mission was launched in 2012 by NASA
